message:
fix(storage,sync): inline LIMIT formatting and preserve external deps during orphan cleanup

src/storage/sqlite.rs — Simplify LIMIT clause generation in three query
builders (list_issues_filtered, a second filtered-list variant, and a
third query path). Previously each site pushed " LIMIT ?" as a
parameterized placeholder, converted the usize limit to i64 via
try_from(...).unwrap_or(i64::MAX) or a bare `as i64` cast (one of
which needed a #[allow(clippy::cast_possible_wrap)]), and appended the
value to the params vec. All three sites are replaced with a single
`write!(sql, " LIMIT {limit}")` call that formats the usize directly
into the SQL string. This is safe because `limit` is a Rust usize —
not user-supplied text — so there is zero SQL-injection surface, and it
eliminates the i64 conversion complexity and the clippy suppression.

src/sync/mod.rs — Fix the JSONL import orphan-cleanup pass so it no
longer deletes external dependency records. The orphan cleaner iterates
a list of (table, column) pairs and DELETEs rows whose foreign-key
column references an issue ID not present in the issues table. External
dependencies use an "external:<url>" synthetic ID that will never appear
in the issues table, so the blanket DELETE was incorrectly purging them.
The fix adds a conditional: when the target is the `dependencies` table
with `depends_on_id` column, the DELETE gains an additional
`AND {col} NOT LIKE 'external:%'` predicate, preserving external dep
rows while still cleaning genuine orphans.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
